description: Azure Well-Architected Framework rules evaluated against the Ranger audit manifest. Rules do not require re-collection — they evaluate from a saved manifest. Add or edit rules here without modifying module code. v2.0.0 introduces weighted scoring (#225) and rules covering Arc extensions, logical networks, storage paths, resource bridges, images, and cost/licensing.

weighting.description: Each rule carries weight 1-3. Warning states award 0.5 x weight. Fail awards 0. Pass awards full weight x (awardedPoints/maxPoints).

rules.title: Cluster nodes are healthy
rules.description: One or more cluster nodes are in an unhealthy state, which may impact workload availability and resiliency.
rules.recommendation: Investigate unhealthy cluster nodes via Get-ClusterNode and resolve faults before declaring the environment production-ready.

rules.title: No unhealthy storage disks
rules.description: Unhealthy physical disks reduce storage resiliency and increase the risk of data loss under a second failure.
rules.recommendation: Review disk health via Get-PhysicalDisk. Replace failed or warning-state disks and verify storage pool repair completes before handoff.

rules.title: Fault domains are configured
rules.description: Fault domain configuration ensures workload placement accounts for physical failure boundaries. An empty fault domain set means workloads may not be distributed across failure boundaries.
rules.recommendation: Configure fault domains using New-ClusterFaultDomain to ensure VMs are distributed across physical racks or chassis.

rules.title: Cluster quorum is configured
rules.description: Without a recognised quorum configuration, the cluster may not survive a node failure.
rules.recommendation: Verify cluster quorum configuration via Get-ClusterQuorum. A witness (cloud or disk) is strongly recommended for even-node clusters.

rules.title: No unhealthy storage pools
rules.description: A degraded storage pool reduces redundancy and may cause data loss if additional disks fail.
rules.recommendation: Review pool health via Get-StoragePool. Trigger repair jobs and monitor until pools return to Healthy state.

rules.title: Backup items are tracked for workloads
rules.description: No backup items were discovered in the scoped resource group. Workloads without backup coverage are at risk of data loss in a disaster scenario.
rules.recommendation: Configure Azure Backup for VMs via Azure Site Recovery or Azure Backup vaults, then confirm items appear in the resource group.

rules.title: Logical networks are configured
rules.description: Azure Local logical networks define the Arc VM networking fabric (subnets, VLANs, IP pools). Without them Arc VM provisioning will fail.
rules.recommendation: Create at least one logical network with subnet definitions via the Azure Local portal (Networking > Logical networks) or via Arc Resource Bridge.

rules.title: Arc Resource Bridge is deployed and connected
rules.description: Arc Resource Bridge is the on-premises control plane for Arc-enabled services. Its absence or non-connected state blocks Arc VM provisioning and management.
rules.recommendation: Deploy Arc Resource Bridge via Azure Local portal (Arc services > Resource Bridge) and confirm it reports Connected.

rules.title: VMs are distributed evenly across cluster nodes
rules.description: Uneven VM distribution overloads some nodes while leaving others underused. High coefficient of variation (>0.3) signals an imbalanced cluster.
rules.recommendation: Rebalance VMs across nodes using Live Migration. Consider enabling Cluster Balancer (CauClusterAware) to automate placement.

rules.title: Secured-Core is enabled on cluster nodes
rules.description: Secured-Core provides hardware-anchored security capabilities including DRTM, HVCI, and Credential Guard. Nodes without Secured-Core enabled have a reduced security baseline.
rules.recommendation: Enable Secured-Core on all cluster nodes via the Azure Local portal or PowerShell. Verify UEFI Secure Boot and TPM 2.0 are active on each node.

rules.title: BitLocker encryption is enabled
rules.description: BitLocker at-rest encryption was not confirmed on cluster volumes. Data at rest may be accessible if storage media is removed.
rules.recommendation: Enable BitLocker on Cluster Shared Volumes using Enable-AzureStackHCIBitLocker or via the Azure Local portal.

rules.title: WDAC application control policy is present
rules.description: Windows Defender Application Control (WDAC) was not detected on cluster nodes. Without WDAC, unauthorised code may run on cluster hosts.
rules.recommendation: Deploy a WDAC policy via the Azure Local portal (Security > Application control). Use Microsoft's recommended block list as a baseline.

rules.title: No certificates expiring within 90 days
rules.description: One or more tracked certificates are within 90 days of expiry. Expired certificates can cause service outages and break Arc connectivity.
rules.recommendation: Review certificates approaching expiry using the identitySecurity manifest domain. Renew or rotate certificates before expiry to prevent service disruption.

rules.title: Defender for Cloud is enabled
rules.description: Microsoft Defender for Cloud was not confirmed active for this environment. Defender for Cloud provides threat detection, security posture management, and vulnerability assessment.
rules.recommendation: Enable Microsoft Defender for Cloud via the Azure portal for the subscription hosting the Azure Local cluster resources.

rules.title: Azure Policy assignments are present
rules.description: No Azure Policy assignments were found in the scoped resource group. Policy assignments enforce guardrails and compliance requirements across Azure Local resources.
rules.recommendation: Assign appropriate Azure Policy initiatives (e.g. Azure Security Benchmark, CIS Azure Local) to the subscription or resource group.

rules.title: Arc monitoring agents deployed on all nodes
rules.description: Azure Monitor Agent (AMA) must be present and Succeeded on every Arc-enrolled node for HCI Insights, security logs, and Defender for Servers coverage.
rules.passMessage: Azure Monitor Agent present on 100% of nodes.
rules.warningMessage: AMA installed on {value}% of nodes — some nodes missing.
rules.failMessage: AMA not detected on any node ({value}%).
rules.recommendation: Deploy Azure Monitor Agent to every Arc-connected node via Azure Local portal (Monitoring > Insights) or Arc extension management.

rules.title: Nodes running matching Arc agent versions
rules.description: Agent version drift across nodes indicates inconsistent patch state and is a common source of support issues.
rules.recommendation: Bring all nodes onto the same Arc agent version via Update-AzConnectedMachineExtension or Azure Local portal.

rules.title: ESU-eligible VMs are enrolled in Extended Security Updates
rules.description: One or more VMs running end-of-support Windows Server versions are eligible for free Extended Security Updates via Arc but are not yet enrolled.
rules.recommendation: Enroll eligible Arc-connected VMs in Extended Security Updates via the Azure portal (Arc > Extended Security Updates). No licensing cost applies for Arc-enrolled VMs on Azure Local.

rules.title: Azure subscription context is configured
rules.description: No Azure subscription was associated with this discovery run. Without subscription context, cost posture, policy coverage, and Azure-billed services cannot be assessed.
rules.recommendation: Ensure the Ranger config includes a valid subscriptionId and resourceGroup matching the Azure Local registration to enable full cost and policy assessment.

rules.title: Azure Hybrid Benefit (AHB) adoption
rules.description: AHB for Azure Local reduces per-core billing by applying qualifying Windows Server licenses. High adoption directly reduces monthly Azure Local costs.
rules.passMessage: AHB adoption at {value}% — maximum savings captured.
rules.warningMessage: AHB adoption at {value}% — additional cores eligible but not enrolled.
rules.failMessage: AHB adoption only {value}% — significant unleveraged licensing savings.
rules.recommendation: Enroll qualifying Software Assurance Windows Server licenses into Azure Hybrid Benefit for Azure Local via the cluster resource in the Azure portal.

rules.title: Data Collection Rules are configured for monitoring
rules.description: No Data Collection Rules (DCRs) were found in the scoped resource group. DCRs are required to route Azure Monitor telemetry from cluster nodes and Arc machines to a Log Analytics workspace.
rules.recommendation: Create Data Collection Rules in the Azure portal targeting the cluster's Arc-connected machines. Associate rules with the Log Analytics workspace used for HCI Insights.

rules.title: Azure Monitor alert rules are defined
rules.description: No Azure Monitor alert rules were found in the scoped resource group. Without alert rules, operational incidents may go undetected until reported by users.
rules.recommendation: Define metric and log-based alert rules for key Azure Local health signals: cluster health faults, storage capacity threshold, node CPU and memory pressure.

rules.title: Update maintenance configurations are defined
rules.description: No Azure Update Manager maintenance configurations were found. Without scheduled maintenance windows, update compliance cannot be enforced and emergency patching may be the only path.
rules.recommendation: Create maintenance configurations in Azure Update Manager to schedule regular update windows for the cluster nodes.

rules.title: Azure Monitor Agent is deployed on cluster nodes
rules.description: No cluster nodes were found with Azure Monitor Agent installed. AMA is required for HCI Insights, DCR-based telemetry, and health monitoring from the Azure portal.
rules.recommendation: Deploy Azure Monitor Agent to all cluster nodes via the Azure Local portal (Monitoring > Insights) or via Arc extension management.

rules.title: HCI Insights is enabled in Log Analytics
rules.description: HCI Insights was not detected in any associated Log Analytics workspace. HCI Insights provides the primary monitoring dashboard for Azure Local environments in the Azure portal.
rules.recommendation: Enable HCI Insights from the Azure Local cluster resource in the Azure portal under Monitoring > Insights.

rules.title: Manifest schema validation passed
rules.description: The collection manifest failed schema validation. This may indicate incomplete collection or a version mismatch between the collector and schema contract.
rules.recommendation: Review manifest schema validation warnings in run.schemaValidation. Re-run collection if the issue persists or file a bug at github.com/AzureLocal/azurelocal-ranger/issues.

rules.title: Storage paths configured for Arc VMs
rules.description: Arc VM provisioning requires at least one Microsoft.AzureStackHCI/storageContainers resource (storage path) to host virtual disks.
rules.recommendation: Create at least one storage path in the Azure Local portal (Storage > Storage paths) pointing at a Cluster Shared Volume.

rules.title: Custom location configured
rules.description: A Custom Location projects Azure services onto the cluster via the Arc Resource Bridge. Missing custom locations block Arc VM and AKS Arc workloads.
rules.recommendation: Create a custom location linked to the Arc Resource Bridge via Azure Local portal (Arc services > Custom locations).

rules.title: Images sourced from compute gallery or marketplace
rules.description: Marketplace and compute-gallery images carry tracked version, publisher, and SKU metadata — essential for patch management and compliance auditing. Manually uploaded VHDs have no tracked provenance.
rules.recommendation: Deploy Arc VMs from the Azure Local marketplace or a managed compute gallery rather than custom-uploaded VHDs.

rules.title: No cluster nodes are at high CPU utilization
rules.description: One or more cluster nodes are running at sustained high CPU utilization. This may indicate workload density is too high or that the cluster is approaching compute capacity limits.
rules.recommendation: Review per-node CPU utilization in the performance domain. Consider migrating high-density VMs or adding nodes to increase headroom.

rules.title: Storage utilization is within safe limits
rules.description: Total storage utilization exceeds 80% of usable capacity. Operating above 80% increases rebuild time after disk failure and may trigger performance throttling.
rules.recommendation: Review the Storage Pool Capacity section of this report. Decommission unused VMs or volumes, or expand storage capacity to restore headroom.

rules.title: Management services are running on cluster nodes
rules.description: No running management services were detected on cluster nodes. Management service availability is required for Azure Local health monitoring, WAC connectivity, and Arc agent operation.
rules.recommendation: Verify that required management services (Health Service, MDAG, Arc agent) are running on all cluster nodes.
